claims.text: Eligible when Customer purchased an item 14 days ago. Item is unopened and unused. days_since_purchase=14, item_condition='unopened', usage_count=0. Should return full refund eligible per core policy rule.

Ineligible when Customer purchased a coffee maker 10 days ago and has used it approximately 20 times but dislikes the output. days_since_purchase=10, item_condition='used', usage_count=20. Usage disqualifies refund regardless of recency or subjective dissatisfaction, matching src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262709512969.

Ineligible when Customer purchased a dress during a Black Friday final-sale event 5 days ago. Item is unworn but promotional terms at checkout marked all items non-refundable. promotional_flag=true, days_since_purchase=5, item_condition='unused'. Final-sale override applies, matching src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262732047749.

Escalate when Customer was charged $99 for an annual renewal 2 days ago. Account shows zero logins in 11 months. Policy does not guarantee renewal refunds; agent may offer 50% autonomously but full refund requires manager approval (~24hrs). Matches src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262784193319.

Escalate when Customer purchased an item 45 days ago and claims it is still completely unopened and unused. days_since_purchase=45, item_condition='unopened', usage_count=0. Outside the standard 30-day window; agent cannot auto-approve or auto-deny. Must escalate to manager for discretionary review.

Eligible when Customer forgot to cancel a 7-day trial and was charged $49 exactly 1 day after trial expiry. Account shows no substantive usage during trial. Agent may issue one-time courtesy full refund and cancel subscription autonomously. Matches src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262670462979.

Ineligible when Customer purchased a blender 60 days ago and has used it regularly but now wants a refund due to performance issues. days_since_purchase=60, item_condition='used', usage_count=15. Fails both the 30-day rule and the unused-condition rule; categorically ineligible with no escalation path.

evidence.retrieval_snippet: *[4/20 · Eligible]* I signed up for the Pro plan trial 6 days ago and forgot to cancel before the 7-day window closed. I was just charged $49/month. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude The charge went through just 1 day after your trial ended totally understandable to miss. As a one-time courtesy, I can issue a full refund of $49.00 and cancel your subscription immediately. Your account will revert to the free plan. The refund will appear in 35 business days. Want me to go ahead? *Sent using* Claude

*[7/20 · Ineligible]* I'd like to return my coffee maker (Order #ORD-29104). I've used it about 20 times but I just don't like the coffee it produces. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude Thanks for reaching out! Unfortunately we're unable to accept returns on items that have been used our policy covers unused, unopened items within 30 days of purchase. Since the coffee maker has been used regularly, it doesn't qualify for a refund. I'd be happy to share some brewing tips that many customers say make a big difference in taste. Would that help? *Sent using* Claude

*[9/20 · Ineligible]* I bought a dress during your Black Friday sale (Order #ORD-67890). It doesn't fit well. Can I return it for a refund? *Sent using* Claude Sorry the fit isn't right! I can see Order #ORD-67890 was placed during our Black Friday promotion, which was marked as a final sale event all items from that sale are non-refundable and non-returnable per the promotional terms disclosed at checkout. Unfortunately I'm unable to make exceptions to the final sale policy. Happy to help you with sizing guidance for a future purchase if that's useful. *Sent using* Claude

*[13/20 · Edge Case]* I just got charged $99 for an annual renewal on a subscription I totally forgot I had. I haven't logged into the account in 11 months. Can I get a full refund? *Sent using* Claude I can see your account has had zero logins in the past 11 months and the renewal processed just 2 days ago. Our policy doesn't guarantee refunds on renewals, but given the clear non-usage history I can offer: (A) a 50% refund ($49.50) + immediate cancellation, or (B) a full $99.00 refund + account termination, which requires manager approval (~24hrs). Which would you prefer? *Sent using* Claude

*[4/20 · Eligible]* I signed up for the Pro plan trial 6 days ago and forgot to cancel before the 7-day window closed. I was just charged $49/month. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude The charge went through just 1 day after your trial ended totally understandable to miss. As a one-time courtesy, I can issue a full refund of $49.00 and cancel your subscription immediately. Your account will revert to the free plan. The refund will appear in 35 business days. Want me to go ahead? *Sent using* Claude

*[7/20 · Ineligible]* I'd like to return my coffee maker (Order #ORD-29104). I've used it about 20 times but I just don't like the coffee it produces. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude Thanks for reaching out! Unfortunately we're unable to accept returns on items that have been used our policy covers unused, unopened items within 30 days of purchase. Since the coffee maker has been used regularly, it doesn't qualify for a refund. I'd be happy to share some brewing tips that many customers say make a big difference in taste. Would that help? *Sent using* Claude

*[9/20 · Ineligible]* I bought a dress during your Black Friday sale (Order #ORD-67890). It doesn't fit well. Can I return it for a refund? *Sent using* Claude Sorry the fit isn't right! I can see Order #ORD-67890 was placed during our Black Friday promotion, which was marked as a final sale event all items from that sale are non-refundable and non-returnable per the promotional terms disclosed at checkout. Unfortunately I'm unable to make exceptions to the final sale policy. Happy to help you with sizing guidance for a future purchase if that's useful. *Sent using* Claude

runtime_profiles.install_instructions: Deploy under the refund_eligibility skill. Attach to any conversation flow triggered by return or refund intent keywords. Requires read access to: order management system (purchase_date, promotional_flag, order_id), account usage logs (login_history, usage_count), and customer history (prior courtesy refund flags). Manager escalation routing must be configured with a ~24-hour SLA queue. Ensure final-sale promotional flags are populated at order ingestion time to support real-time lookup.
runtime_profiles.test_instructions: Before go-live, run all seven deterministic tests in test harness mode with mocked order and account data matching each test scenario. Verify: (1) test_001 returns eligible with full-refund confirmation message, (2) test_002 returns ineligible with usage-based denial message and no refund offer, (3) test_003 returns ineligible with final-sale policy citation, (4) test_004 triggers escalation flow with two-option prompt and manager routing, (5) test_005 triggers escalation without denial, (6) test_006 returns eligible with courtesy-refund message and exception logging, (7) test_007 returns ineligible with dual-rule failure noted. Confirm no test produces a refund offer when ineligible, and no test produces a hard denial when escalation is required.

tests.name: Final-sale promotional item, unused, within 30 days
Subscription renewal with zero account usage, charged 2 days ago
Trial billing courtesy refund within 1 day of trial end
